title: Quality Gate Orchestration, Failure Semantics, and Required Checks · Delivery-System Trace: Ci Gate Evidence Summary Ownership
summary: A pipeline runs formatter, lint, type, tests, and build under one continue-on-error step, reports green after skipped work, and protects the branch with a mutable job name. Planned artifact: Add and verify the ci gate evidence summary ownership increment in quality gate dependency graph. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Publish concise annotations and summaries with exact command, revision, environment, failure owner, retained artifact, and rerun limits.
Design stable job names, required status checks, merge queues, branch rules, bypass authority, and migration without accidental deadlock.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example pipeline hides formatter, lint, type, test, and build failures behind continue-on-error and skipped jobs while branch rules require a renamed check. Rebuild the dependency graph and evidence ownership. Competency probe: Publish concise annotations and summaries with exact command, revision, environment, failure owner, retained artifact, and rerun limits. A defensible solution applies Ci Gate Evidence Summary Ownership, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to quality gate dependency graph.

content.code:
# Worked focus: ci-gate-evidence-summary-ownership — Publish concise annotations and summaries with exact command, revision, environment, failure owner, retained artifact, and rerun limits.
  # Evidence: ci-worked-ci-quality-gate-orchestration-2
  - competency: ci-worked-ci-quality-gate-orchestration-2
    artifact: quality-pipeline.yaml
    decision: worked ci quality gate orchestration 2
    environment: trusted release tag requiring an environment approval
    fault-injection: route five percent traffic to a revision with a data incompatibility
    retained-proof: accessible redacted timeline with owner expiry and regression control
    case-key: worked2
    change: |
      gates: [format-check, lint, type-check, unit, integration, build, policy]
      order: cheap-before-costly
      parallel: independent-only
      continue-on-error: false
      skipped-policy: explicit-failure
      required-check: stable-quality-name
      summary: command-revision-owner
      rerun: classified-only
    verify: inspect job results required checks skipped paths exact commands and retained failure summaries
    failure: Reject green continued failures, silent skips, mutable required names, log dumps, or unconditional reruns.
    repair: Make exit semantics explicit, preserve dependency order, fail unintended skips, stabilize checks, and assign diagnostic ownership.
content.caption: Runnable CI/CD workflow, artifact, release, and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
